status_enum.description: Single canonical health status vocabulary. All organs emit these values from /health, /ready, and /attest endpoints.

required_endpoints.purpose: Liveness probe. Tiny, fast, dependency-light. Returns canonical status_enum value.
Readiness to serve canonical tool surface. Includes registry truth and session substrate health.
Identity attestation. Returns organ_id, role, version, tool_count, schema_hash, identity_anchor, health_status, attestation_status, attested_at, expires_at.
Canonical contract endpoint. Returns supported protocol versions, required headers, error envelope schema.
MCP Streamable HTTP endpoint. JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP POST with session management.

required_headers.traceparent: W3C trace context for distributed tracing

optional_headers.mcp-session-id: MCP session identifier from initialize handshake

surface./mcp: JSON-RPC 2.0 error object with standardized error.data fields

data.jsonrpc_code: Numeric JSON-RPC error code (-32600 to -32603 reserved)
data.attestation_status: attestation status at time of error
data.upstream_status: HTTP status from upstream if applicable

conformance_spine.gate_rule: No substrate-grade claim may be made unless all 9 conformance steps pass. No wisdom claim unless spine is green AND attestation is fresh AND registry truth holds.

transport_contract_lock.description: Every advertised port, path, protocol version, and tool count matches canonical registry

transport_health_enum.description: /health emits only canonical statuses. Compatibility aliases accepted on input, never emitted

transport_jsonrpc_errors.description: Error responses preserve JSON-RPC id, valid error structure, typed fault_code

transport_problem_details.description: REST non-MCP errors use application/problem+json with stable problem types

retry_deadline_budget.description: Retries use randomized exponential backoff, bounded budgets, deadline propagation

dependency_cascade_drill.description: All organs survive upstream slowness without false green

hold_never_void.description: Mechanical faults never convert to VOID; only constitutional faults

metadata.status: RATIFIED — effective for all federation organs
metadata.supersedes: All ad-hoc port registries, health vocabularies, and protocol version hardcodings in individual repos
